title: Honeymoon

jokes.text: This was a good choice. Yep. We make good choices. This was a really healthy choice.
jokes.setup: Post-coital conversation where they're trying to convince themselves
jokes.punchline: The overly emphatic repetition of 'good choice' reveals their uncertainty
jokes.explanation: Strong character-based comedy showing their awkwardness through repetitive self-reassurance. Economy is excellent - says everything with minimal words.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That was a good choice.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Really good choice.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Very good choice.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Excellent choice.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Best choice we've ever made.

jokes.text: What? It's your dad. He saw me. Fuck! It's the towels again, we need towels!
jokes.punchline: The absurd priority of towels over the embarrassing situation
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from panic to mundane concern. Shows David's character through his priorities even in crisis.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my god, oh my god, oh my god. We need to get out of here before anyone sees us.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: David, relax. No one saw anything.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No one saw anything? Stevie, I am literally standing here in a towel that says 'Property of the Rosebud Motel' on it.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Okay, but that's not even one of our nice towels.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's your concern right now?

jokes.text: Okay, this is a face cloth, and a bath mat.
jokes.punchline: Stevie's deadpan delivery of clearly inadequate substitutes
jokes.explanation: Stevie's dry delivery makes the absurdly inadequate towel substitutes funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Do you have any towels?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, we have some old t-shirts and a bath mat from 2003.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's not... those aren't towels.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, they absorb water, so technically...

jokes.text: Are you in danger? Blink, if you're in danger.
jokes.setup: Johnny hearing suspicious sounds from the closet
jokes.punchline: The overly dramatic hostage negotiation approach to a simple situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and character integration. Johnny's protective dad instincts go comically overboard. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Hello? Is someone in there?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm going to need you to come out slowly. Keep your hands where I can see them.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We can work through this. Whatever you're feeling right now, I understand.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, it's just the cat.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David, please. I'm in the middle of a very delicate negotiation.

jokes.text: Well, I, uh... I've got everything I need. Good seeing you both.
jokes.setup: Johnny realizing what he's walked into
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to gracefully exit while processing the situation
jokes.explanation: Johnny's polite Canadian awkwardness in an uncomfortable situation is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I... I see. Well. That's... that's very... I'm just going to... yes. I'll just be... somewhere else. Anywhere else. Right now.

jokes.text: So, was it cute for you, or what? It was fine. It was... It was weird. It was good. Love that. It was good but weird. I love that!
jokes.setup: Alexis being overly enthusiastic about David's hookup
jokes.punchline: David's contradictory description and Alexis's inappropriate excitement
jokes.explanation: Great sibling dynamic - Alexis's inappropriate enthusiasm contrasts with David's confusion about his own experience.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, David, so you're finally getting back out there! I'm so excited for you!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's really not a big deal. He's just someone I met. It probably won't go anywhere.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That's so hot! When are you seeing him again?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not sure we will be. He seemed very interested in cryptocurrency.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's amazing! You have so much in common!

jokes.text: Ted wants to meet my friends, so I think it's getting kind of serious. It's been two weeks.
jokes.setup: Alexis thinking two weeks is serious
jokes.punchline: David's deadpan reality check
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Alexis's romantic naivety vs David's cynicism. Economical and sharp.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Two weeks? That's serious.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, I've been with Patrick for two years.

jokes.text: Because a podunk baby out of wedlock is not something your father and I can afford to negotiate at this moment.
jokes.punchline: Moira's characteristically pretentious and clinical way of describing an unplanned pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira voice - turning a simple concept into theatrical, clinical language. 'Negotiate' is particularly brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: An unplanned pregnancy is simply a contraceptive failure manifesting as a nine-month inconvenience with a lifetime of financial and emotional ramifications.

jokes.text: Well, that's crazy, because all I want is some podunk baby out of wedlock!
jokes.punchline: Alexis sarcastically using Moira's exact phrase back at her
jokes.explanation: Great immediate callback with Alexis throwing Moira's pretentious phrase right back at her sarcastically.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not pregnant, so I guess I'm not a 'plague of locusts descending upon the family.'

jokes.text: I think that you two really, actually need the idea of, um, getting a life.
jokes.setup: Alexis building up to serious criticism of her parents
jokes.punchline: The blunt 'get a life' delivered in her valley girl style
jokes.explanation: Alexis's hesitant delivery of a harsh truth, showing her character development while still being funny.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm just going to say it. You guys need to get a life.

jokes.text: Uh, walking over to the cafe and ordering a tuna melt is hardly what I would call a 'flourishing social life.'
jokes.setup: Johnny claiming they have a flourishing social life
jokes.punchline: Alexis's specific, mundane example of their actual activities
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor with specific detail ('tuna melt') making it more vivid and relatable.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yeah, like last week, we went to the grocery store and then we came home and David microwaved some salmon.

jokes.text: Well, now I don't remember. Yeah, I don't remember.
jokes.setup: Being confronted by their children's criticism
jokes.punchline: Both parents simultaneously 'forgetting' what they came to discuss
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing how the tables have turned - the parents are now the ones being defensive.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, Dad, you can't just—

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You know what? I suddenly have no idea what we were talking about.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Mm, same. Completely blank.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You literally just walked in here—

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Nope. Gone.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Vanished. Like my first marriage.

jokes.text: You know, the more I think about it, the more I wonder whether David's whole sexually adventurous thing was just a phase. It's not a phase.
jokes.punchline: Johnny's immediate, firm correction
jokes.explanation: Shows the parents' different levels of understanding and Johnny's protective instincts for his son.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, you know, David has always been a bit ambiguous.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: He's not ambiguous. He's gay.

jokes.text: That was just a... Was a one-time thing. That was a one time... Just a blip... Just a blip. Blop. Just a...
jokes.punchline: The awkward fumbling with words, especially 'blop'
jokes.explanation: Great awkwardness with the made-up word 'blop' showing how flustered they both are.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So what was that exactly?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I don't know, what do you want to call it?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, it wasn't... I mean, we didn't...

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Right, so it was just a...

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: A blop?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Did you just say 'blop'?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I don't know what we're doing here, Stevie.

jokes.text: Blippity blop. Blippity blip.
jokes.setup: David leaving after the awkward conversation
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on the nonsense words as an exit strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - making his awkward exit even more awkward with made-up words. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I'm going to go... skooch myself to the store. Very skooch. Much skooch energy.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Don't wait up. I'll be back when I'm back. Or whenever. Time is a flat circle of skooch.

jokes.text: You know that if it's in your dreams, or over the clothes, it's not cheating, right?
jokes.punchline: His specific, oddly innocent definition of what constitutes cheating
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mutt character moment - naive but delivered with complete confidence. The specificity makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: So what would you consider cheating in a relationship?

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Because I think if you're using someone else's hiking trail without asking, that's basically cheating.

jokes.text: Event. Oh, it's more of a soiree.
jokes.punchline: Roland's pretentious correction to 'soiree' for what's clearly a backyard barbecue
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Roland's pretensions about what's clearly a simple barbecue.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: It's not a barbecue, it's a soiree.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Roland, there are hot dogs on a card table.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Yes, well, a sophisticated card table.

jokes.text: Pineapples, pig on the spit, the whole nine yards. Oh, you, uh, roast pigs? We do! Sure do. We have a big one hanging in the garage.
jokes.punchline: The contrast between 'soiree' and having a pig hanging in the garage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from fancy 'soiree' to the crude reality of a pig in the garage.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: We're having a soiree.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: A what?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: A soiree. It's French for a fancy evening party.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, we can't have a soiree. We've got a pig hanging in the garage.

jokes.text: Hoity-toity. All right, I've said it.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain why they didn't invite the Roses
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's blunt admission after dancing around the issue
jokes.explanation: Great payoff after the buildup - Jocelyn's no-nonsense honesty cuts through the politeness.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Well, we just... we didn't think you'd want to come.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: You're not really our people.

jokes.text: I like the wine and not the label.
jokes.punchline: Perfect summation of his sexuality using wine terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline to the wine metaphor. Economical, character-defining, and beautifully written. One of the best lines in the series.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm like a wine that's hard to pair. Some people think I'm too dry, others find me too sweet, and most people just get a headache.

jokes.text: No, no, no, I absolutely did that. Just wept for hours in the dark.
jokes.setup: Stevie asking if he cried himself to sleep with regret
jokes.punchline: David's matter-of-fact confirmation with dramatic detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect David response - dramatic but delivered deadpan. Shows his emotional honesty in a funny way.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I did, actually. I cried myself to sleep, and then I woke up at 3 a.m. in a panic, and then I cried myself back to sleep.

jokes.text: So this whole room... Just gets delivered, as is. This is the 'bachelor's den' here, and then that's the 'executive dining lounge'
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous pretentious names for his pre-fab room sets
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ted's earnestness about his obviously tacky furniture setup.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: This is the 'Scandinavian minimalist' collection. It's actually just a bed frame and a nightstand from a big box store, but it sounds better when you say it that way.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: And this one here is the 'mid-century modern restoration' — it's particle board, but the legs are angled, which apparently makes all the difference.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: My personal favorite is the 'contemporary farmhouse aesthetic.' It's literally just wood painted white with some metal handles. Cost me forty dollars.

jokes.text: Oh, I should take the rolls out of the microwave. Hot rolls!
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic announcement about microwaved rolls
jokes.explanation: Shows Ted's earnest but amateur hosting skills - microwaved rolls presented with genuine enthusiasm.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Everyone, I just want to say that these rolls are fresh from the microwave!

jokes.text: Twyla, I love your outfit, you look like a waitress!
jokes.punchline: The unintentionally insulting comparison since Twyla IS a waitress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - trying to be nice but revealing her obliviousness to others' actual lives.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You're like the perfect waitress.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, you're so good at it, you could actually be a waitress.

jokes.text: I just wanna wipe my hands all over that! You just keep getting funnier. I'm not joking though, I really do.
jokes.punchline: Her inappropriate touching desire and Johnny's misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Jocelyn's inappropriate behavior and Johnny's polite confusion.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: That suit is absolutely gorgeous. I just want to touch it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, thank you. It's a Tom Ford.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: No, I mean I want to touch *you* in it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh! Well, that's... that's not appropriate.

jokes.text: Now, listen. I'm the guy in charge of upholding the law here, so if you have any ideas about turning me in, you'd just be turning me in to myself.
jokes.punchline: His circular logic about being both the lawbreaker and law enforcer
jokes.explanation: Excellent Roland logic - corrupt but presented as if it makes perfect sense. Great character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, I smoke a little marijuana. What are you gonna do, arrest me? I'm the town council.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'm also the law. So if I arrest myself, that's an abuse of power by me against me.

jokes.text: Spark it up, Roley! Spark it up.
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's overly enthusiastic drug encouragement using a cutesy nickname
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Jocelyn's inappropriate enthusiasm, though not particularly original or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Go ahead, Rollie! Don't be shy!

jokes.text: We had friends in the FDA, but... When in Rome...
jokes.punchline: Moira's casual reference to FDA connections and her philosophical acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - casually revealing their past corruption while adapting to new circumstances.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, I have a very close relationship with the FDA. My dermatologist's cousin works there. So I'm comfortable with my choices.

jokes.text: The judicial system is really cracking down on its delinquents these days, huh?
jokes.setup: Mutt and Twyla's community service was washing a car
jokes.punchline: David's sarcastic observation about the incredibly light 'punishment'
jokes.explanation: Good David snark that highlights the absurdity of small-town justice while staying in character.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Washing a car. Yes, that's a real punishment. I'm sure they're devastated. Next week, perhaps they'll be forced to organize a closet. The week after that, maybe fold some linens. Truly, the justice system has spoken.

jokes.text: Nothing. I mean, we talk, but... We don't really talk about anything. Like, nothing.
jokes.setup: David asking what she and Mutt talk about
jokes.punchline: Twyla's earnest explanation of talking about nothing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the genuine but inarticulate relationship between Twyla and Mutt.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Well, we just... talk about nothing really. Like, he'll say something about the weather, and I'll say something back about the weather, and then we'll just kind of stand there and look at each other for a while.

jokes.text: Thank you, Stevie. I ordered it online from a restaurant supply place. No sales tax.
jokes.punchline: Ted's proud revelation that he bought pre-made food online for tax savings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ted moment - earnestly proud of his practical but unromantic approach to dinner party hosting.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, thank you. I actually ordered it all online from a restaurant supply company.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: It's a tax write-off if it's for business purposes.

jokes.text: What if I had not been crowned Dairy Princess and moved to the city with my driving instructor?
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's specific and ridiculous alternate life scenario
jokes.explanation: Great specific detail that reveals Jocelyn's past and romantic naivety. 'Dairy Princess' is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: What if I had become a professional yodeler in the Swiss Alps? I'd have three ex-husbands, all named Klaus, and a pet chamois named Mr. Whiskers who was also my accountant.

jokes.text: Who's the eyebrows buying everyone's drinks?
jokes.punchline: Referring to Roland by his prominent eyebrows
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character detail - reducing Roland to his most prominent feature in a memorable way.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I met Roland at a... well, it was a real estate seminar, and he had these eyebrows that just... they came into the room before he did.

jokes.text: It was funny? Yeah, it was a... It was a fun... It was a funny... It was... Yes.
jokes.setup: Stevie asking if their hookup was funny
jokes.punchline: David's increasingly flustered attempt to clarify what he meant
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David digging himself deeper with poor word choice.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Was it funny?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What? No. I mean, yes. I mean, not in a bad way. I was laughing because I was happy, not because anything was actually... I wasn't laughing at you.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: David.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm just saying, the laughing was contextual. It was a happy laugh. A satisfied laugh.

jokes.text: I bet nobody thought sex with Trixie was funny.
jokes.setup: Stevie being offended about David calling their encounter 'funny'
jokes.punchline: Her comparison to Twyla's prostitute friend
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback from Stevie using the inappropriate dinner conversation against David.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm not funny. I'm not trying to be funny. What you're describing sounds like something that would happen to Twyla's friend who charges by the hour.

jokes.text: My son is pansexual. I've heard of that. I know what that is, that's, uh, that cookware fetish.
jokes.punchline: Roland's complete misunderstanding confusing it with kitchenware
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup and perfect Roland response. The misunderstanding is both innocent and absurd. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David's gay.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, David's gay? That's wonderful! So he's got one of those fancy European kitchens?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, Roland, he's homosexual.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh! A homo... sexual... is that like a home sexual? Does he like having sex at home?

jokes.text: But, you know, just pick one gender, and stick with it. Maybe everything would've been less... Confusing.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to be supportive of David
jokes.punchline: His well-meaning but problematic suggestion to 'pick one gender'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's love for David mixed with his generational confusion about sexuality.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David, I think what you need to do is just pick one gender and stick with it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, I'm not confused about my gender.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, then what's the problem?

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I just had to get us out of that party. It was like being in an episode of Couples Therapy.
jokes.punchline: Stevie's specific comparison to reality TV therapy
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the awkwardness, with a specific cultural reference that lands.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: It's like watching a therapy session on reality TV, except everyone involved is actually terrible at communicating, and there's a good chance someone's going to cry into their wine.

jokes.text: Well, you deserve a Daytime Emmy for that performance.
jokes.setup: Stevie pretending to be upset during dinner
jokes.punchline: David's backhanded compliment using the least prestigious Emmy category
jokes.explanation: Great David line that's both complimentary and insulting. The Daytime Emmy reference is perfectly snobbish for his character.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, Stevie, I have to say, you're taking this remarkably well. I mean, for someone of your... limited acting range, this is quite the performance.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Thank you?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: In fact, I'd say you're Emmy-worthy. Outstanding Guest Actress in a Short Form Comedy or Drama Series worthy.

jokes.text: Not the fact that you're sleeping with a girl, but, you know, I'd be just as happy if you were to bring home one of your guy friends.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to be supportive of David's sexuality
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to show acceptance while making it weird
jokes.explanation: Good Johnny character moment - his heart is in the right place but his execution is painfully awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: David, I want you to know that I'm very supportive of your... lifestyle. Very supportive. I've been reading about it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Dad, you don't need to read about it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I want to understand. I'm very open-minded. Very progressive. You know, some of my best friends are... well, they're not, but they could be.

jokes.text: Grass, too much grass. Hey, track that cycle, missy!
jokes.punchline: Admitting to being high and then giving reproductive advice
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing high Johnny getting even more inappropriate with his parenting advice.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm not drunk, I'm high. And let me tell you something about reproduction — it's overrated. Don't do it.

tmdb_overview: Johnny and Moira attend Roland’s annual Hawaiian bash. Alexis and Ted throw a couple’s dinner party, while David and Stevie’s relationship heats up.
insight: Season finale balances character depth with 41 jokes—their strongest cringe-irony blend yet.
